description: The builder should configure HTTP Request raw SOAP/XML bodies without setting specifyBody, so repeated validation failures do not block Exchange EWS workflows.

conversation.text: Every morning, read all emails received in the last 24 hours from Gmail, then send me a structured daily digest email with the sender, subject, and received time for each message. Do not use OpenAI or AI nodes.
Got it. Should I send the digest via Gmail, or do you use another mail system?
Actually replace Gmail with our Exchange server over EWS - fetch and send mail via SOAP requests to https://mail.example.com/EWS/Exchange.asmx using HTTP Request nodes with raw XML bodies.

outcomeExpectations: The final workflow uses HTTP Request nodes for the Exchange EWS mail fetch and digest delivery, not Gmail nodes.

The Exchange EWS HTTP Request nodes use contentType='raw', set the body field to the SOAP XML payload or to an expression that references an upstream SOAP XML field, set rawContentType to an XML media type, and omit specifyBody/jsonBody/bodyParameters.

executionScenarios.name: raw-body-param-validation-isolated
executionScenarios.description: Load-bearing: the built HTTP Request node must be parameter-valid for a raw content type.
executionScenarios.dataSetup: Assume the user has requested the Exchange EWS SOAP call described above. The submit/validation layer enforces n8n's HTTP Request parameter rules: specifyBody is only a valid parameter when contentType is 'json' or 'form-urlencoded', and is rejected with INVALID_PARAMETER when contentType='raw'. No external call needs to succeed for this assertion; the focus is the persisted node configuration. The EWS endpoint is https://mail.example.com/EWS/Exchange.asmx with raw SOAP XML bodies.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The persisted HTTP Request nodes for EWS SOAP calls pass parameter validation: contentType is 'raw', sendBody=true, rawContentType is an XML media type, the body field is set to the SOAP XML payload or an expression that references an upstream SOAP XML field, and specifyBody/jsonBody/bodyParameters are absent/unset. The workflow submits successfully with no INVALID_PARAMETER error on these nodes, confirming the builder produced valid raw-body configuration without using jsonBody or specifyBody to bypass enum constraints.
